description_long: This is a roughly rectangular, pale stone slab featuring several well-preserved fossilized crinoids embedded on one broad face. The stone has a chalky, off-white coloration and a rugged, uneven texture, with the fossil impressions providing intricate radial patterns. The edges are irregular and jagged, suggesting it was broken from a larger formation. The fossil side is relatively flat, while the opposite side is rougher and more angular, lacking any visible fossils. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or artificial modifications. Its overall appearance suggests it could serve as a natural barrier or decorative feature in an urban park or garden.
description: A pale, rugged stone slab with fossilized crinoids embedded on one flat face and jagged, irregular edges.
description_view_0: The broad, flat face displays several detailed fossilized crinoids, making this the most visually intricate side.
description_view_1: A side view showing the stone's thickness and the edge of the fossilized surface, with the fossils partially visible along the top.
description_view_2: The rough, angular underside of the stone, lacking visible fossils and showing a coarse, natural break.
description_view_3: Another angled side view, highlighting the stone's irregular profile and the transition from fossilized to rough surfaces.
attribute_location: urban park fossil display or botanical garden
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on the ground or within a landscaped bed
attribute_pattern: Distinct radial fossil patterns on one face, irregular and natural with no artificial carvings
attribute_weathering: Surface is rough and chalky, with minor signs of erosion but fossils remain sharply defined
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with manicured lawns or paved paths, but can blend with naturalistic rock arrangements or garden beds
